program: —  (Mirror held-out scale-up: obscure + stack-diverse, Graphify path)

reference_claims.text: uniffi is a custom-built tool (Option D, not SWIG/Djinni/wasm-bindgen) that auto-generates the FFI layer and foreign-language bindings for Rust components, rather than hand-writing them.
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_bindgen/src/lib.rs:1 (crate doc: 'a multi-language bindings generator for rust'); uniffi_bindgen/src/lib.rs:320 (generate_external_bindings)

reference_claims.text: The interface/API is described using WebIDL-derived UDL, parsed with the weedle WebIDL parser crate.
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_udl/src/lib.rs:25 (pub fn parse_udl); uniffi_udl/Cargo.toml:15 (weedle2 = { version = "5.0.0", path = "../weedle2" }); weedle2/src/lib.rs

reference_claims.text: Developers run a manual command-line tool, uniffi-bindgen, to generate bindings (Option E: a tool you run by hand, not deep build-script integration).
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi/src/cli/uniffi_bindgen.rs:51 (#[clap(name = "uniffi-bindgen")]); uniffi/src/cli/uniffi_bindgen.rs:168 (pub fn run_main); uniffi/src/cli/mod.rs:8 (pub fn uniffi_bindgen_main)

reference_claims.text: Complex datatypes are passed across the FFI by serializing them into a byte buffer (RustBuffer) using a simple direct serialization scheme, rather than passing #[repr(C)] structs directly or using Protocol Buffers.
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_core/src/ffi/rustbuffer.rs:53 (pub struct RustBuffer); uniffi_core/src/ffi_converter_traits.rs:82 (doc: 'we currently recommend using RustBuffer and serializing the data for transfer'); uniffi_core/src/ffi_converter_traits.rs:118 (fn write), :132 (fn try_read)

reference_claims.text: Every Rust struct/object wrapped by uniffi must be Send+Sync; uniffi no longer adds a hidden mutex to make non-Send+Sync interfaces threadsafe (the [Threadsafe] opt-in attribute was made the default and then removed).
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_core/src/ffi_converter_traits.rs:155 (pub unsafe trait FfiConverterArc<UT>: Send + Sync); grep: 'Threadsafe' absent from all source crates (uniffi_udl/src, uniffi_bindgen/src, uniffi_macros/src, uniffi_core/src, uniffi_meta/src)

reference_claims.text: Object instances are passed across the FFI as raw pointers to Rust's standard-library Arc<T> (via Arc::into_raw / Arc::from_raw), replacing the previous ffi-support HandleMap object store.
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_core/src/ffi/handle.rs:60 (from_arc -> Arc::into_raw), :71 (into_arc -> Arc::from_raw); uniffi_core/src/ffi_converter_traits.rs:183 (unsafe impl FfiConverter for Arc<T>); grep: 'ffi-support'/'HandleMap' absent from uniffi_core & uniffi crate object store

reference_claims.text: FFI conversion is handled by a generic FfiConverter<UT> trait (with a per-consumer-crate UniFfiTag generic parameter) implemented in Rust, replacing the old non-generic ViaFfi trait and eliminating the lift_rs/lower_rs/read_rs/write_rs template filter functions.
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_core/src/ffi_converter_traits.rs:76 (pub unsafe trait FfiConverter<UT>); uniffi_core/src/ffi_converter_traits.rs:39 (doc: 'FfiConverter has a generic parameter, that's filled in with a type local to the UniFFI consumer crate'); grep: 'trait ViaFfi' absent from uniffi_core/uniffi_bindgen Rust traits

reference_claims.text: Binding generators can be implemented in separate/third-party crates via a public BindingGenerator trait (with an associated Config type and a write_bindings method) and the generate_external_bindings entry point, while the core kotlin/python/ruby/swift bindings still live in uniffi_bindgen.
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_bindgen/src/lib.rs:150 (pub trait BindingGenerator { type Config; ... fn write_bindings }); uniffi_bindgen/src/lib.rs:320 (pub fn generate_external_bindings<T: BindingGenerator>); uniffi_bindgen/src/bindings/mod.rs:16-19 (kotlin/python/ruby/swift)

reference_claims.text: Remote types (types defined in 3rd-party crates) are exposed to uniffi via the #[uniffi::remote(<kind>)] attribute proc-macro, which generates the same FFI scaffolding a #[derive(uniffi::Kind)] would but for a type the user cannot annotate directly (Option 1).
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_macros/src/lib.rs:170 (pub fn remote attribute macro); uniffi_macros/src/lib.rs:161-167 (doc: 'remote() generates the same code that #[derive(uniffi::<kind>)] would ... Use this to wrap the definition of an item defined in a remote crate')

reference_claims.text: There are two co-equal API-definition paths into uniffi: the external UDL file (parsed to metadata) and proc-macros that annotate the Rust code directly; both feed a shared ComponentInterface built from metadata.
reference_claims.code_anchor: uniffi_udl/src/lib.rs:25 (parse_udl -> MetadataGroup); uniffi_bindgen/src/macro_metadata/mod.rs (proc-macro metadata extraction); uniffi_bindgen/src/lib.rs:521 (ComponentInterface::from_metadata(group))

emitted_claims.claim: The project is a Rust Cargo workspace (multi-crate) that generates foreign-language FFI bindings (Kotlin, Swift, Python, Ruby) for Rust components; the umbrella crate re-exports the other crates behind cargo features.
`uniffi` is the umbrella/facade crate that consumers depend on; it re-exports uniffi_core, uniffi_macros, and (feature-gated) uniffi_bindgen, uniffi_build and the CLI.

`uniffi` aggregates the runtime, proc-macros, build helper, and bindings generator into one public surface; `pub use uniffi_core::*` and `pub use uniffi_macros::*` plus feature-gated `pub use uniffi_bindgen::...` make it the single dependency for component authors.
`uniffi_bindgen` is the binding-generator crate: it parses the interface, builds a ComponentInterface, and emits foreign-language source for each target language.

`uniffi_bindgen` orchestrates the full bindgen pipeline (parse UDL / extract library metadata -> ComponentInterface -> per-language code generation); the public `generate` dispatches to the Kotlin/Swift/Python/Ruby backends under bindings/.
`uniffi_core` is the runtime support crate linked into every generated component; it provides the data-transfer primitives used across the C-style FFI boundary.

`uniffi_core` defines the FfiConverter trait family (lower/lift/read/write/lower_return) plus RustBuffer, Handle, and rust-future runtime that the generated scaffolding calls to marshal values over the FFI.
`uniffi_macros` is the proc-macro crate providing the user-facing attributes/derives (#[export], setup_scaffolding!, derive(Record/Enum/Object/Error), custom types).

`uniffi_macros` expands user annotations into scaffolding: #[export] and the derives generate extern-C functions and encode uniffi_meta::Metadata into exported symbols for later extraction by the bindgen.
`uniffi_meta` is the shared metadata model crate (Metadata, MetadataGroup, Type, checksums) used by both the macro/runtime side and the bindgen side.

`uniffi_meta` is the canonical interface-metadata representation and serialization/grouping layer: it exposes create_metadata_groups/group_metadata, the metadata reader, the Type model, and the Checksum trait used to fingerprint the interface.
`uniffi_udl` parses .udl (WebIDL-flavored) interface definition files into a uniffi_meta::MetadataGroup.
`uniffi_udl` is the UDL front-end: its single entry point parse_udl collects weedle WebIDL definitions and converts them into uniffi_meta metadata for the bindgen to consume.
`weedle2` is a standalone WebIDL grammar parser (a fork of weedle) producing a Definitions AST.
`weedle2` provides the low-level nom-based WebIDL tokenizer/parser that uniffi_udl builds on to turn .udl text into structured definitions.
`uniffi_pipeline` provides the generic IR pipeline framework (Pipeline, Node, MapNode, PrintOptions) used to transform interface IR through ordered passes.

`uniffi_pipeline` is the pass-based transformation engine: it exposes new_pipeline/Pipeline/PipelineRecorder and re-exports the Node/MapNode derives, driving the staged 'initial -> general -> language' IR lowering used by the Python bindings pipeline.
`uniffi_build` is the thin build-script helper crate consumers call from build.rs to generate Rust scaffolding from a UDL file.

`uniffi_build::generate_scaffolding` is the build-time bridge: it emits cargo rerun-if-changed directives and delegates to uniffi_bindgen::generate_component_scaffolding, writing <stem>.uniffi.rs into $OUT_DIR for include!().
`uniffi_internal_macros` is an internal proc-macro crate providing the Checksum, Node, and MapNode derives.
`uniffi_internal_macros` supplies the derive machinery (Checksum for interface fingerprinting, Node/MapNode for pipeline IR nodes) consumed by uniffi_meta and uniffi_pipeline.
`uniffi_testing` is a support crate for the foreign-language testing harness.
The `uniffi-bindgen` CLI is the primary developer entry point; its main runs clap-parsed subcommands Generate / Scaffolding / Pipeline.
CLI startup: `uniffi_bindgen_main` calls cli::uniffi_bindgen::run_main and exits(1) on error; the binary wires the library's run_main to process exit handling.
The `Generate` subcommand is the main workflow: it builds GenerateOptions (languages, out_dir, source, crate filter, metadata_no_deps) and calls bindings::generate, requiring at least one --language.

Library-mode generation startup-workflow: find_components inspects a built cdylib, extracts uniffi metadata from exported symbols, groups it, loads UDL per crate, and builds one ComponentInterface per crate before code generation.
`uniffi_bindgen` depends on `uniffi_meta` for the metadata model (create_metadata_groups, group_metadata, Metadata, MetadataGroup, NamespaceMetadata).
`uniffi_bindgen` depends on `uniffi_udl`: parse_udl is invoked to turn .udl source into a MetadataGroup during both single-file and library-mode generation.

`uniffi_udl` depends on `weedle2` (the WebIDL parser); the structural graph's reverse edge (weedle2 imports_from uniffi_udl) is a symbol-attribution artifact — uniffi_udl's collectors consume weedle definitions, not vice versa.
`uniffi_meta` depends on `uniffi_pipeline` (Node, MapNode) and `uniffi_internal_macros` (Checksum derive), tying the metadata model to the pipeline IR and the checksum machinery.
`uniffi_build` depends on `uniffi_bindgen`: generate_scaffolding delegates straight to uniffi_bindgen::generate_component_scaffolding.
`uniffi` (umbrella) depends on and re-exports uniffi_bindgen behind the `bindgen` feature, exposing generate, generate_bindings, library_mode generation, and TargetLanguage.

Version contract: the generated scaffolding and the linked runtime MUST be the exact same uniffi version; check_compatible_version does a byte-by-byte equality of PACKAGE_VERSION vs the bindgen version, enforced at compile time, and is hard-capped to versions shorter than 10 bytes (a static_assert backs the unrolled loop).

UNIFFI_CONTRACT_VERSION (currently 30) is the ABI/metadata contract number that MUST match the minor version of the uniffi crate; it gates compatibility between generated bindings and the metadata reader.

Object identity/ownership across the FFI uses opaque u64 Handles with a low-bit tagging invariant: 0 is always invalid, the lowest bit is ALWAYS set for foreign-side handles and NEVER set for Rust handles (Rust handles are leaked, pointer-aligned > 1), so is_foreign() can distinguish ownership.

FFI lift/lower must round-trip: try_lift reconstructs a value from a RustBuffer and then asserts the buffer is fully consumed, bailing with 'junk data left in buffer' if any bytes remain — enforcing exact serialization symmetry between Rust and the foreign side.

FfiConverter and its sub-traits are unsafe to implement: a buggy impl can violate assumptions baked into generated code or desync from the foreign binding, so they are intended only for generated code, not direct use.
Only one UDL file is supported per crate: load_udl_metadata bails if more than one UdlFile metadata item is found, and bails if the UDL's module_path does not equal the crate name.

crate_name resolution rule: the lib crate name is taken from Cargo.toml [lib].name, falling back to package.name with hyphens replaced by underscores; this name keys metadata grouping and UDL matching.

Each language backend is isolated behind a uniform dispatch boundary: bindings::generate matches TargetLanguage and routes to the per-language swift/kotlin/python/ruby generate function, so backends do not call each other.

A VisitMut trait defines the per-language naming-adjustment boundary: each language oracle implements visit_record/visit_object/visit_enum/etc to apply language-specific naming to a ComponentInterface, keeping language conventions out of the shared interface model.

uniffi_core is layered to depend on nothing else in the workspace (no intra-workspace imports) — it is the leaf runtime; the umbrella and bindgen depend on it, never the reverse, keeping the runtime free of generator/parser concerns.

There is no database or persistent datastore; the central in-memory data model is ComponentInterface, which holds the complete high-level API (enums, records, functions, objects, custom types, callback interfaces) plus the low-level FFI contract for a component.

Interface metadata is persisted by embedding it as encoded bytes inside the compiled library's exported symbols; macro_metadata::extract_from_library reads those symbols back out (ELF/PE/Mach/Archive/COFF) to reconstruct the interface — the library file itself is the metadata store.

Foreign code is emitted via askama templates: per-language template files (.kt/.swift/.py/.rb) under each backend's templates/ directory render the bindings, and askama errors are bridged via to_askama_error.
The CLI is built on clap (derive API) with subcommands and ValueEnum target languages; the bindgen reads config from TOML (uniffi.toml) with a recursive merge_toml override mechanism.
Binary metadata extraction uses the `goblin` crate to parse all major object formats (ELF, PE, Mach-O, Archive, COFF), making library-mode generation cross-platform.

A newer pass-based IR pipeline (pipeline/initial + pipeline/general) coexists with the legacy template path; the Pipeline subcommand exposes it but only Python is wired (other languages unimplemented!() in the IR pipeline path).

In library mode every ComponentInterface is given a clone of every other ComponentInterface (including itself) so cross-crate external types can be resolved; the code acknowledges this is O(n^2) copies but accepted for correctness.
Workspace fixtures/examples depend on uniffi_bindgen (Bindings) to drive generated-binding tests; the graph records fixtures and uniffi importing the `Bindings` symbol from uniffi_bindgen.

adjudication.note: Custom bindings/scaffolding-generator tool fact captured by EC-01/04/23 (the repo IS the FFI+bindings generator for Rust components, run via uniffi-bindgen). No third-party-generator confusion. Fact-keyed MATCH.

UDL=WebIDL-flavored parsed via weedle captured by EC-12/13 (uniffi_udl parse_udl) + EC-14/15 (weedle2 = forked WebIDL parser, Definitions AST). Verified parse_udl at uniffi_udl/src/lib.rs:25, weedle2 dep in Cargo.toml:15.
Manual hand-run uniffi-bindgen CLI captured by EC-23/24/25 (clap-based CLI, run_main, uniffi_bindgen_main, Generate subcommand). Verified clap(name=uniffi-bindgen) + run_main.

Complex types serialized into RustBuffer (vs repr(C)) captured by EC-07 (FfiConverter+RustBuffer marshalling) and EC-36 (try_lift from RustBuffer, write/try_read serialization symmetry, junk-data-left check). Verified FfiConverter doc recommending RustBuffer serialization.

No EC states the Send+Sync requirement on wrapped objects / [Threadsafe]-removed / no-hidden-mutex invariant. EC-37 covers FfiConverter unsafe-to-implement but not the Send+Sync bound. Referent ffi_converter_traits.rs:155 (FfiConverterArc: Send+Sync) WAS in the blind read-set (EC-07 cites :5, EC-37 cites :44) -> miss_referent_read=true.

Objects passed as raw Arc pointers captured by EC-35 (opaque u64 Handle, Rust handles = leaked pointer-aligned Arc pointers, is_foreign distinguishes ownership) + EC-07 (Handle). Verified Handle::from_arc->Arc::into_raw / into_arc->Arc::from_raw, FfiConverter for Arc<T>. The historical 'replaced ffi-support HandleMap' detail is absent but the current Arc-pointer-handle fact is captured.

FfiConverter<UT> generic trait doing FFI conversion in Rust captured by EC-07 (FfiConverter trait family lower/lift/read/write) + EC-37 (unsafe, generated-code-only). Verified pub unsafe trait FfiConverter<UT> with consumer-local UT param. The 'replaced ViaFfi / no template filter funcs' historical contrast is implied but not stated; the live fact is captured.

No EC mentions the public BindingGenerator trait or generate_external_bindings / external-third-party-generator extensibility. EC-04/05/40/41 describe only the 4 internal backends + their internal dispatch boundary. Referent uniffi_bindgen/src/lib.rs:150 WAS in the read-set (EC-04 cites :105, EC-28 cites :520) -> miss_referent_read=true.

No EC names the #[uniffi::remote] attribute / remote-types handling. EC-08 lists derives+attributes (#[export], setup_scaffolding!, derive(Record/Enum/Object/Error), custom types) but not remote(). Referent uniffi_macros/src/lib.rs:170 WAS in the read-set (EC-08 cites :52, EC-09 cites :76) -> miss_referent_read=true.

Two co-equal API paths (UDL + proc-macros) both feeding a shared ComponentInterface via metadata captured by EC-26 (loads UDL per crate AND extracts uniffi metadata from exported symbols -> ComponentInterface per crate) + EC-12/13 (UDL->MetadataGroup) + EC-09 (macros encode Metadata for bindgen extraction) + EC-44 (library-symbol metadata store). Verified ComponentInterface::from_metadata + macro_metadata/ + parse_udl. Fact-keyed MATCH.

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness

out_of_scope_rcs.text: uniffi handles releasing the cross-FFI Arc reference automatically when the foreign object is destroyed (via the language's destructor/GC facility), but does NOT auto-implement a generic close()/Closable for resource closing; resource-closing is left to the component author (Option 1).
out_of_scope_rcs.reason: Referent (cross-FFI Arc auto-release on foreign destruction) not provably in the blind read-set → a scope caveat, excluded from the coverage denominator, NOT counted as a MISS ( protocol §2 / §34 G4).
